title: Roku Q1 2023 Earnings Summary - TheWrap
text: Roku exceeded expectations in its first quarter of 2023, reporting stronger-than-anticipated results despite ongoing economic challenges. The streaming platform generated $741 million in revenue, surpassing the company's forecast of approximately $700 million. This represented a 1% increase year-over-year. The company posted a net loss of $193.6 million, or $1.38 per share—narrower than the projected $1.37 loss per share that analysts had anticipated. Platform revenue, derived primarily from advertising and subscription partnerships, reached $635 million but declined 1% compared to the prior year. Device revenue showed stronger momentum, rising 18% to $106 million. Roku expanded its user base by 1.6 million active accounts, bringing the global total to 71.6 million. The company noted that "In the U.S., our Active Accounts are approaching half of all broadband households," highlighting its market penetration. Average revenue per user declined 5% to $40.67, while streaming engagement hit record levels with users averaging "3.9 streaming hours per active account per day." Roku acknowledged persistent macroeconomic headwinds, stating that "the total U.S. advertising market down 7.4% year over year." However, certain sectors like travel and wellness improved, offsetting weakness in media and financial services. For Q2, management projected $770 million in revenue with continued negative adjusted EBITDA of approximately $75 million.
